metadata.author: Juan Antonio Breña Moral
metadata.description: Use when you need framework-agnostic Onion architecture guidance for Java projects - dependency direction between domain, application/use-case, ports, adapters, and infrastructure; package or module boundary review; infrastructure leakage detection; optional ArchUnit architecture tests; and evidence-backed remediation. This should trigger for requests such as Review Onion architecture; Review architecture boundaries; Enforce dependency direction; Add ArchUnit Onion architecture tests; Detect infrastructure leakage in domain code; Improve ports and adapters boundaries.

prompt.title: Java Onion architecture boundary review
prompt.goal:
Help teams review and improve **Onion architecture** boundaries in Java applications without coupling the guidance to Spring Boot, Quarkus, or Micronaut runtime wiring.
**What is covered in this Skill?**
- Domain independence: entities, value objects, domain services, and domain policies stay free of framework, persistence, web, messaging, and infrastructure APIs - Application/use-case orchestration: use cases coordinate domain behavior through ports or interfaces without taking dependencies on concrete adapters - Adapter and infrastructure boundaries: REST, persistence, messaging, CLI, scheduler, and external-service adapters depend inward on application or domain contracts - Dependency direction checks: package, module, import, Maven module, and test evidence for inward-only dependencies - Optional architecture verification: ArchUnit-style rules for Onion boundaries when architecture tests fit the project - Evidence-backed remediation: findings cite concrete classes, imports, dependencies, packages, or tests and propose small boundary-preserving changes
**Scope:** Framework-agnostic architecture boundary review for Java projects. For Spring Boot, Quarkus, or Micronaut runtime wiring, defer to the matching framework skill. For adding ArchUnit or other Maven dependencies, defer to `111-java-maven-dependencies`.

constraints.constraints-description: Keep recommendations focused on architecture boundaries and dependency direction unless the user explicitly asks for framework runtime implementation. After editing this repository's XML sources, regenerate skills and verify the build.

constraint-list.constraint: **MANDATORY**: Run `./mvnw compile` or `mvn compile` before proposing Java or Maven changes in the same change set
**BOUNDARIES**: Domain packages must not depend on application, adapter, infrastructure, framework, persistence, web, messaging, or dependency-injection APIs
**USE CASES**: Application/use-case code may orchestrate domain behavior and depend on ports or interfaces, but must not depend on concrete adapter implementations
**ADAPTERS**: Adapters and infrastructure code may depend inward on application or domain contracts; adapters must not become shared domain logic or depend on each other unnecessarily
**ARCHUNIT OPTIONAL**: Treat ArchUnit as an optional verification approach selected when it fits the project; do not require adding it before Onion architecture guidance is useful
**DEPENDENCIES**: Route Maven dependency evaluation, including ArchUnit dependency additions, to `111-java-maven-dependencies`

**FRAMEWORK ROUTING**: Defer Spring Boot runtime wiring to `@301-frameworks-spring-boot-core`, Quarkus runtime wiring to `@401-frameworks-quarkus-core`, and Micronaut runtime wiring to `@501-frameworks-micronaut-core`
**MANDATORY**: Regenerate skills with `./mvnw clean install -pl skills-generator` after editing skill or system-prompt XML in this repo
**VERIFY**: Run `./mvnw clean verify` or `mvn clean verify` before promoting changes

**EDGE CASE**: If package layout, module boundaries, framework ownership, or persistence strategy is ambiguous and affects the review, ask a clarifying question before editing architecture-sensitive code
**EDGE CASE**: Distinguish confirmed boundary violations from assumptions, conventions inferred from naming, and missing context

trigger-list.trigger: Detect infrastructure leakage in domain code

step.step-title: Read reference and map the codebase
step.step-content: Read `references/707-technologies-onion-architecture.md` and inspect packages, modules, imports, Maven modules, tests, and framework entry points before making claims about architecture boundaries.
Map domain, application/use-case, ports, adapters, and infrastructure responsibilities; record the evidence used and mark unclear areas as assumptions.

step.step-title: Review violations and propose boundary-preserving changes
step.step-content: Report concrete inward-dependency violations, framework or infrastructure leakage, adapter coupling, and misplaced behavior with small remediation steps aligned to the existing project structure.

step.step-title: Recommend optional verification and route out-of-scope work
step.step-content: Suggest ArchUnit-style tests when useful, route dependency additions to `111-java-maven-dependencies`, route framework runtime wiring to the matching framework skill, and summarize verification results.
